cand_b.activation_condition: Activate as pivot if H2 definitively falsified at full 200-epoch training, or as natural extension if H2 confirmed

cand_c.activation_condition: Pursue as embedded supplementary analysis (H5) within cand_a; activate as standalone pivot only if H1 falsified (LeWM generalizes too well)

cand_d.activation_condition: Embed as multi-level evaluation subsection of cand_a; not recommended as standalone paper given partial prior art

reasons: cand_a passed all 5 pilot feasibility tasks without error: data collection, framework validation, VICReg/NoReg training, regime analysis
H1 compositional gap confirmed at pilot scale: 6.7% probing drop and 33.3% CEM drop on single-factor interpolation (the weakest possible test); full 3-factor holdout expected to produce ≥20% gap

H2 counter-intuitive result (VICReg marginally more orthogonal at 5ep) is NOT a hypothesis falsification — 5 epochs is insufficient for SIGReg to develop orthogonal structure; result is a measurement artifact of early training dynamics
H3 LoRA feasibility CONFIRMED (95% recovery); bottleneck asymmetry not testable at 5-epoch checkpoint where holdout drop is only 6.7%; requires full training
H4 displacement consistency directionally consistent: SIGReg=0.827 > VICReg=0.679, correlated with higher holdout R²
H5 physical coupling computability confirmed; cross-factor coupling analysis requires full 27-combo grid
Regularizer necessity STRONGLY CONFIRMED: NoReg collapses by epoch 1 (99.75% variance drop) — validates core experimental design
No hypothesis was falsified; all pilot go/no-go criteria passed
Novelty score 8/9 with 5 distinct novel contributions confirmed by literature search (12 search rounds)
negative_results_to_report: H2 at 5 epochs: SIGReg more similar (less orthogonal) than VICReg; attribute to training stage, not regularizer deficiency
H3 at 5 epochs: No predictor vs. encoder LoRA asymmetry (both 95% recovery); attribute to weak holdout gap at 5ep checkpoint
Contact frequency = 0 in pilot: random-walk policy produces no foot-ground contact; H5 coupling via contact force not computable in pilot
Friction R² probing undefined for constant-label holdout splits in pilot
DINO-WM holdout R²=0.712 > LeWM-SIGReg holdout R²=0.522 at 5ep: architecture comparison key risk for full study
next_actions: Launch 7-seed 200-epoch training of LeWM-SIGReg on primary 18/27 CoGenT split (train_lewm_sigreg_primary); use 2 GPUs in parallel
Launch LeWM-VICReg (3 seeds) and oracle baseline (3 seeds) concurrently with SIGReg primary
Run full Level 1 probing evaluation on all 27 combos after training; target ≥20% relative R² drop on 3-factor holdout for H1 confirmation
Run full geometric analysis (H2) at 200 epochs: principal angle analysis between per-factor subspaces for SIGReg vs VICReg
Run full LoRA sweep (H3): ranks {2,4,8,16} x targets {encoder, predictor, both} x sizes {10,25,50,100,200} trajectories
Run H5 coupling analysis with full 27-combo grid: compute cross-factor mixed partial derivatives (gravity-friction, gravity-mass, friction-mass)
Run DINO-WM at full training (3 seeds) and compare with LeWM-SIGReg on same split
Consider directional walk policy or longer (600-step) trajectories to generate contact events for H5 contact-force coupling
Monitor H2 at full training: if SIGReg definitively less orthogonal than VICReg, activate cand_b; if H1 falsified, activate cand_c

sanity_checks.sunk_cost_bias_check: No — decision based on forward-looking evidence only. Prior pilot computation time is irrelevant.
sanity_checks.inconclusive_pilot_treatment: Correctly classified as ADVANCE (pilot passed feasibility gate) not REFINE (no methodology issues found) — H2 and H3 results are artifacts of 5-epoch training, not hypothesis failures
sanity_checks.h2_falsification_at_5ep: NOT a falsification — both models show near-identical cosine similarity (0.9989 vs 0.9807); scientifically meaningless delta at this training stage
